# Transcrição: 10. Dont't Be THAT Guy

- **Fonte:** 10. Dont't Be THAT Guy
- **Duração:** 02:12
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:12]

Don't be that guy. Don't be that guy. You know the guy I'm talking about. 48% of salespeople never ever ever follow up a prospect. Not one call. Not a email. Not a piece of mail. Nothing. Not even a thank you for coming to visit us. Not even, hey thanks for showing an interest. 48% of all salespeople feel so freaking sick about their job, what they do, where they work, themselves that they would never contact somebody and say hey thanks for coming over here, thanks for showing an interest, thanks for the shot, the opportunity. What's wrong with you man? What are you doing? Like you got you got to get you got to get some integrity in your career and yourself to say wait a minute I can't I can't run my business like this. You're running a company with no people. What are you doing? You can't do everything if you're the sole owner of the company and you have no people to do the follow-up and your your excuses I don't have time then hire people. 48% of all salespeople and sales organizations never bother to follow up a prospect. Go to a bank, an insurance company, a real estate company. Go anywhere. Go into a get a massage or call for a haircut or get your nails done or send your kids to school or to karate or a special class. Nobody ever calls. Ever. Give some money to the Republican Party or give it to the Democrats. Hey they might they might actually follow you up. Why? Because they want some more of that money. They understand follow up follow up follow up nurture the lead work the lead stay in touch with the lead. They do a tremendous job in politics to follow people up but at the retail level we don't. You actually give somebody something. You give them a good service a good product and we don't call them back. This is a self-esteem issue I'm telling you for you to be in your business and feel good about it you want to know this I'm not one of the 48% I at least call my customer back the first time but here's the problem if you only call them back one time there's no chance they're gonna buy from you you will not be different than anyone else in the market except for the lower 48% don't be that guy
